rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CANDLESTICK HEIGHTS COMMUNITY ALLIANCE, AN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery on Friday, June 13, 2025 Line 1, PETITIONER CANDLESTICK HEIGHTS COMMUNITY ALLIANCE, AN UNINCORPORATED ASSOCIATION's Motion For Writ Of Mandate. Hearing required. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MELODY STUDER ET AL VS. PETER H. BRESLER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike And/Or Tax Defendants' Memorandum Of Costs In Trial Court
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery on Friday, June 13, 2025 Line 2, PLAINTIFF MELODY STUDER, ANGELA ULE's Motion To Strike And/Or Tax Defendants' Memorandum Of Costs In Trial Court. Plaintiffs Melody Studer and Angela Ule's motion to strike and/or tax trial court costs claimed by defendants is granted in part. Judge Karnow has retired and is not available to decide this motion. $240 of the filing and motion fees, $1,081.40 of the deposition costs for deponent Peter Bresler and expert Terry Lloyd, and all $1,388 of the fees for electronic filing or service are stricken for a total of $2,709.40. All other claimed costs are allowable. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RYAN ANTHONY MORALES VS. WILLIE ARDERY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Compelling Plaintiff To Sign Medical Record Authorizations; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery on Friday, June 13, 2025 Line 3, DEFENDANT EXECUTIVE HOTEL VINTAGE COURT, LLC's Motion For Protective Order Compelling Plaintiff To Sign Medical Record Authorizations; Request For Sanctions. Defendant Executive Hotel Hotel Vintage Court, LLC's motion to compel plaintiff Ryan Morales to sign medical record authorizations is denied. Defendant fails to provide the court with either the authorization he wants plaintiff to sign or the subpoena it issued to Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital, so the court has no way of knowing whether the proposed authorization or the subpoena seeks only discovery relevant documents. Sanctions requests by both sides are denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) rulings.Case Title: MAHER MEMARZADEH VS. ANDREW NICHOLAS DIMITRIOU ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery on Friday, June 13, 2025 Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT MAHER MEMARZADEH's DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-defendant Maher Memarzadeh's demurrer to the First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") is overruled. On a demurrer the court takes properly pleaded facts as true. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) In assessing whether the complaint states a cause of action, the court accepts all properly pleaded material facts, but not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. (Minton v. Dignity Health (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 1155, 1161.) "[I]f, on consideration of all facts stated, it appears that plaintiff is entitled to any relief against defendant, the complaint will be held good, though facts may not be clearly stated, or may be intermingled with a statement of other facts irrelevant to cause of action shown, or though plaintiff may demand relief to which he is not entitled under facts alleged." (Augustine v. Trucco (1954) 124 Cal.App.2d 229, 236, quoting Matteson v. Wagoner (1905) 147 Cal. 739, 742.) The court liberally construes the complaint per CCP 452. The FACC pleads sufficient ultimate facts to support the claims for breach of contract and quantum meruit. Cross-defendant improperly challenges the veracity of the allegations. Cross-defendant argues that he retained "Dimitriou & Associates" and therefore "Andrew Dimitriou" is not a proper party to this case. But the fee agreement attached to the cross-complaint does not clearly demonstrate whom cross-defendant retained. Cross-defendant raises factual issues that cannot be resolved on demurrer. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery on Friday, June 13, 2025 Line 9, PLAINTIFF BLUE SKY FOREVER's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment Granted. rulings.Case Title: SAM ZAID VS. GETAROUND, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery on Friday, June 13, 2025 Line 10, PLAINTIFF SAM ZAID AN INDIVIDUAL's APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. Plaintiff Sam Zaid's application for a right to attach order and issuance of a writ of attachment is denied. Mr. Zaid has not shown that any of his claims are probably valid or that the amount he seeks to have secured by the attachment is fixed or readily ascertainable. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
